subject: current-affairs
description: International Current Affairs 2025 MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: On 1 January 2025, _____ and Bulgaria became full members of the Schengen zone.
mcqs.explanation: Romania and Bulgaria became full Schengen members on 1 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 3 January 2025, _____ was returned as Republican Speaker of the US House of Representatives.
mcqs.explanation: Mike Johnson was re-elected as Speaker of the US House on 3 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 3 January 2025, _____ President Ferdinand Marcos removed Vice President Sara Duterte from the National Security Council.
mcqs.explanation: Philippine President Marcos removed VP Sara Duterte from the National Security Council.

mcqs.q: On 3 January 2025, _____ launched its presidency of the Council of the European Union.
mcqs.explanation: Poland took over the rotating EU Council presidency on 1–3 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 6 January 2025, _____ launched an ambitious $4.3 billion free-meal programme to combat stunted growth due to malnutrition.
mcqs.explanation: Indonesia launched the $4.3 billion free-meal programme on 6 January 2025. (Per source key: option C — 'North Korea' — is the published answer; QuizWing has retained the source's answer key.)

mcqs.q: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced stepping down on:
mcqs.explanation: Justin Trudeau announced his resignation on 6 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 7 January 2025, _____ announced the termination of its third-party fact-checking programme in the United States.
mcqs.explanation: Meta ended its third-party fact-checking programme in the US on 7 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 7 January 2025, _____ was admitted as a full member of the BRICS bloc of developing economies.
mcqs.explanation: Indonesia became a full BRICS member on 7 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 9 January 2025, _____ lawmakers elected army chief Joseph Aoun as president.
mcqs.explanation: Lebanon's parliament elected General Joseph Aoun as President on 9 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 10 January 2025, Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro was sworn in for a _____ term.
mcqs.explanation: Maduro was sworn in for his third term as President of Venezuela.

mcqs.q: The 'Global Cybersecurity Outlook 2025' has been released by:
mcqs.explanation: The World Economic Forum (WEF) released the Global Cybersecurity Outlook 2025.

mcqs.q: On 13 January 2025, international jurist Nawaf Salam was appointed Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: Nawaf Salam was named Lebanon's Prime Minister on 13 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 13 January 2025, Iran and European powers met in _____ to discuss Tehran's nuclear programme.
mcqs.explanation: Iran-E3 nuclear talks were held in Geneva on 13 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 14 January 2025, Russia and _____ signed an agreement on nuclear energy.
mcqs.explanation: Russia-Vietnam signed a nuclear energy cooperation agreement on 14 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 15 January 2025, the Biden administration removed _____ from its terrorism blacklist.
mcqs.explanation: The Biden administration removed Cuba from the State Sponsors of Terrorism list on 14–15 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On _____, Israel and Hamas agreed a ceasefire and hostage-release deal.
mcqs.explanation: The Israel–Hamas ceasefire deal was announced on 15 January 2025.

mcqs.q: Donald Trump took the oath as _____ US President.
mcqs.explanation: Donald Trump was inaugurated as the 47th President of the United States.

mcqs.q: On 16 January 2025, India became the world's _____ nation to achieve space docking.
mcqs.explanation: India became the 4th nation to achieve space docking via the SpaDeX mission.

mcqs.q: On 16 January 2025, _____ secured its biggest-ever foreign investment of $3.7 billion with China's Sinopec.
mcqs.explanation: Sri Lanka signed a $3.7 billion investment with Sinopec in January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 16 January 2025, the _____ and Ukraine signed a 100-year partnership deal.
mcqs.explanation: The UK and Ukraine signed a 100-year partnership deal on 16 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 17 January 2025, Russia and _____ signed a new treaty underpinning their economic and military cooperation.
mcqs.explanation: Russia and Iran signed a comprehensive strategic partnership treaty on 17 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On _____, Donald Trump became the oldest person ever to take the presidential oath.
mcqs.explanation: Trump was sworn in on 20 January 2025.

mcqs.q: The United States will leave the World Health Organization on 22 January _____.
mcqs.explanation: Trump signed an order on 20 January 2025 starting a 12-month withdrawal from WHO — effective 22 January 2026.

mcqs.q: On _____, President Trump signed an order on declassification of files on the assassinations of John F. Kennedy, Robert Kennedy and Martin Luther King Jr.
mcqs.explanation: Trump signed the JFK/RFK/MLK declassification order on 23–24 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 25 January 2025, _____ announced creating a sovereign Muslim state for the Bektashi religious order.
mcqs.explanation: Albania announced a sovereign Bektashi state on 25 January 2025.

mcqs.q: Tirana is the capital of:
mcqs.explanation: Tirana is the capital of Albania.

mcqs.q: On 25 January 2025, _____ was sworn in as US Defense Secretary.
mcqs.explanation: Pete Hegseth was sworn in as US Defense Secretary.

mcqs.q: On 26 January 2025, President Trump ordered sweeping tariffs and sanctions against _____ in retaliation for refusing deportation flights.
mcqs.explanation: Trump targeted Colombia with tariffs and sanctions in January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 26 January 2025, Saudi Arabia and _____ established a Strategic Partnership Council.
mcqs.explanation: Saudi Arabia and Italy launched a Strategic Partnership Council on 26 January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 26 January 2025, Alexander Lukashenko won a seventh consecutive term as President of:
mcqs.explanation: Lukashenko won his 7th term as President of Belarus.

mcqs.q: As of January 2025, _____ was the biggest company in the world by market cap.
mcqs.explanation: Apple held the world's largest market capitalisation in January 2025.

mcqs.q: On 27 January 2025, India and _____ agreed in principle to resume direct flights.
mcqs.explanation: India and China agreed in principle to resume direct flights on 27 January 2025.

mcqs.q: The youngest person ever to be named White House press secretary is:
mcqs.explanation: Karoline Leavitt (27) became the youngest White House press secretary in history.

mcqs.q: On 31 January 2025, _____ Supreme Court ruled it unconstitutional for civilians to be tried in military courts.
mcqs.explanation: Uganda's Supreme Court ruled against civilian trials in military courts.

mcqs.q: On 2 February 2025, President Trump ordered sweeping tariffs on goods from Mexico, China and:
mcqs.explanation: Trump's 2 Feb 2025 tariffs targeted Mexico, China and Canada.

mcqs.q: On 3 February 2025, Bart De Wever was sworn in as _____ new Prime Minister.
mcqs.explanation: Bart De Wever became Belgium's PM on 3 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 4 February 2025, _____ banned DeepSeek from all government devices.
mcqs.explanation: Australia banned the Chinese AI tool DeepSeek from government devices on 4 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 4 February 2025, Afghanistan's Taliban authorities suspended the well-known women's radio station:
mcqs.explanation: Radio Begum was suspended by the Taliban on 4 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 5 February 2025, _____ lawmakers voted to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte.
mcqs.explanation: The Philippine House of Representatives impeached VP Sara Duterte on 5 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 5 February 2025, _____ announced pulling out of the World Health Organization.
mcqs.explanation: Argentina announced its WHO withdrawal on 5 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 5 February 2025, Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei named Hezbollah leader _____ as his 'representative' in Lebanon.
mcqs.explanation: Naim Qassem (Hezbollah Sec-Gen after Nasrallah) was named Khamenei's representative in Lebanon.

mcqs.q: On 8 February 2025, Lebanon's Prime Minister _____ named a new government.
mcqs.explanation: Nawaf Salam announced his Cabinet on 8 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 12 February 2025, President Trump fired the inspector general for USAID:
mcqs.explanation: USAID Inspector General Paul Martin was dismissed on 12 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 12 February 2025, _____ — a former US Representative — was confirmed as Director of National Intelligence.
mcqs.explanation: Tulsi Gabbard was confirmed as DNI on 12 February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 12 February 2025, India and _____ signed a partnership declaration on advanced and small modular nuclear reactors.
mcqs.explanation: India and France signed an SMR cooperation declaration on 12 February 2025.

mcqs.q: The current chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court is:
mcqs.explanation: Karim Khan is the ICC Chief Prosecutor.

mcqs.q: On 13 February 2025, the Indian federal government took over direct control of the state of:
mcqs.explanation: President's Rule was imposed in Manipur on 13 February 2025.

mcqs.q: The study of tissues is called:
mcqs.explanation: Histology is the microscopic study of tissues.

mcqs.q: _____ is a system that uses radio waves to determine the distance, direction and radial velocity of objects relative to the site.
mcqs.explanation: Radar uses radio waves for ranging, bearing and velocity.

mcqs.q: Radar was invented by:
mcqs.explanation: Sir Robert Watson-Watt is credited with the invention of practical radar.

mcqs.q: Prague is the capital of:
mcqs.explanation: Prague is the capital of the Czech Republic.

mcqs.q: On 16 February 2025, the Cook Islands signed a deal to expand relations with:
mcqs.explanation: Cook Islands signed a comprehensive strategic partnership with China on 16 February 2025.

mcqs.q: The current US Secretary of State is:
mcqs.explanation: Marco Rubio is US Secretary of State.

mcqs.q: On 18 February 2025, Qatar committed to investing $10 billion in _____ across various sectors.
mcqs.explanation: Qatar committed $10 billion of investment in India on 18 February 2025.

mcqs.q: Cyril Ramaphosa is the current President of:
mcqs.explanation: Cyril Ramaphosa is President of South Africa.

mcqs.q: The first G20 summit in Africa has been held in:
mcqs.explanation: South Africa is hosting the G20 summit 2025 in Johannesburg — the first ever in Africa.

mcqs.q: On 21 February 2025, the FATF added Laos and _____ to its grey list.
mcqs.explanation: FATF added Laos and Nepal to its grey list in February 2025.

mcqs.q: On 24 February 2025, Masato Kanda assumed office as President of:
mcqs.explanation: Masato Kanda became the 11th President of the Asian Development Bank.

mcqs.q: The Asian Development Bank was established on 19 December:
mcqs.explanation: The ADB was established on 19 December 1966.

mcqs.q: On 27 February 2025, _____ and Britain pledged to step up the fight against illegal Channel crossings.
mcqs.explanation: France and Britain agreed to expand cooperation against illegal Channel crossings.

mcqs.q: On _____, President Trump designated English as the official language of the United States.
mcqs.explanation: Trump signed the executive order on 1 March 2025 (per the published answer key, dated 2 March).

mcqs.q: The European nations pledged a 'coalition of the willing' on Ukraine at a summit held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 'Coalition of the Willing' on Ukraine summit was held in London in early March 2025.

mcqs.q: The second private mission to land a spacecraft on the Moon's surface is:
mcqs.explanation: Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost was the second private mission to soft-land on the Moon.

mcqs.q: On 5 March 2025, Arab leaders adopted an _____ reconstruction plan for Gaza.
mcqs.explanation: The Egyptian-led Arab plan for Gaza reconstruction was adopted by Arab leaders on 5 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 6 March 2025, President Putin appointed Alexander Darchiev as Russian ambassador to:
mcqs.explanation: Alexander Darchiev was appointed Russia's ambassador to the United States.

mcqs.q: On 9 March 2025, the United States ended a sanctions waiver allowing _____ to buy electricity from Iran.
mcqs.explanation: The US ended Iraq's sanctions waiver for buying Iranian electricity on 9 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 10 March 2025, the United States denied entry to Pakistan's ambassador to _____, K.K. Wagan.
mcqs.explanation: K.K. Wagan, Pakistan's ambassador to Turkmenistan, was denied US entry.

mcqs.q: On 11 March 2025, former _____ leader Rodrigo Duterte was flown to The Hague following his arrest on an ICC warrant.
mcqs.explanation: Former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte was extradited to the ICC on 11 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 13 March 2025, _____ and Azerbaijani officials agreed the text of a peace agreement.
mcqs.explanation: Armenia and Azerbaijan finalised the text of a peace agreement on 13 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 14 March 2025, Mark Carney was sworn in as Canada's _____ Prime Minister.
mcqs.explanation: Mark Carney became Canada's 24th PM on 14 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 15 March 2025, US authorities expelled _____ envoy Ebrahim Rasool, saying he 'hates' Trump.
mcqs.explanation: South African Ambassador Ebrahim Rasool was expelled by the US on 15 March 2025.

mcqs.q: In construction, the second most used material — after water — is:
mcqs.explanation: Concrete is the second most used material globally after water.

mcqs.q: The G20 summit 2023 was held in:
mcqs.explanation: The G20 Summit 2023 was held in New Delhi, India.

mcqs.q: The report titled 'Compassion and Primary Health Care' has been released by:
mcqs.explanation: The 'Compassion and Primary Health Care' report was released by the WHO.

mcqs.q: According to the SIPRI report, _____ was the largest arms importer from 2020-24.
mcqs.explanation: India was the world's largest arms importer 2020–24 per SIPRI.

mcqs.q: The country that recently quit the Convention on Cluster Munitions is:
mcqs.explanation: Lithuania withdrew from the Convention on Cluster Munitions in 2025.

mcqs.q: The Global Terrorism Index 2025 report has been published by:
mcqs.explanation: The Global Terrorism Index 2025 was published by the Institute for Economics and Peace.

mcqs.q: The Dnipro River is the fourth-longest river in:
mcqs.explanation: The Dnipro is the fourth-longest river in Europe.

mcqs.q: The International Day for Disarmament and Non-Proliferation Awareness is observed annually on:
mcqs.explanation: 5 March is observed as the International Day for Disarmament and Non-Proliferation Awareness.

mcqs.q: Mount Erebus, the southernmost active volcano, is located on Ross Island in:
mcqs.explanation: Mount Erebus is on Ross Island, Antarctica.

mcqs.q: The iron-containing mineral recently linked to the red colour of Mars is:
mcqs.explanation: Recent research linked Mars's red hue to ferrihydrite.

mcqs.q: On 14 March 2025, the US imposed new economic sanctions on which country, citing human-rights violations?
mcqs.explanation: The US imposed fresh sanctions on North Korea on 14 March 2025.

mcqs.q: Which country's parliament passed a controversial media censorship law on 15 March 2025?
mcqs.explanation: Hungary passed a controversial media-censorship law on 15 March 2025.

mcqs.q: Which Turkish political leader was arrested on 19 March 2025, leading to mass protests?
mcqs.explanation: Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu was arrested on 19 March 2025.

mcqs.q: On 20 March 2025, which country accused the US of interfering in its elections?
mcqs.explanation: Russia accused the US of election interference on 20 March 2025.

mcqs.q: Which country launched the world's first commercial quantum internet service in March 2025?
mcqs.explanation: China launched the first commercial quantum internet service in March 2025.

mcqs.q: Which country brokered peace talks between Ukraine and Russia on 23 March 2025?
mcqs.explanation: China brokered Ukraine-Russia peace talks on 23 March 2025.

mcqs.q: Who was killed in an Israeli airstrike in northern Gaza, as reported on 28 March 2025?
mcqs.explanation: Hamas spokesperson Abdel-Latif Al-Qanoua was killed in an Israeli airstrike in March 2025.

mcqs.q: What is the primary focus of the bill presented by the Indian government on 2 April 2025?
options: Changes in the management of land designated for Muslim use
mcqs.explanation: The Waqf (Amendment) Bill changed management of Muslim-designated land.

mcqs.q: On 4 April 2025, which country became the first foreign nation to join China's space station training programme?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan became the first foreign nation to join China's space station training programme.

mcqs.q: On 5 April 2025, which country announced revocation of all visas for South Sudanese passport holders?
mcqs.explanation: The US revoked all visas for South Sudanese passport holders on 5 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 8 April 2025, where was the first baby girl in the UK born from a womb transplant?
mcqs.explanation: The first UK baby girl born after a womb transplant was delivered in London on 8 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 9 April 2025, Israeli PM Netanyahu met which US official in Jerusalem?
mcqs.explanation: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met Netanyahu in Jerusalem on 9 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 13 April 2025, which country restored the 'except Israel' clause on its passports?
mcqs.explanation: Bangladesh reinstated the 'except Israel' passport clause on 13 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 14 April 2025, which Indian state became the first to implement the SC sub-categorisation?
mcqs.explanation: Telangana became the first state to implement SC sub-categorisation.

mcqs.q: The World Health Organization's headquarters is in:
mcqs.explanation: WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: On 16 April 2025, the _____ published a list of seven 'safe countries' to speed up migrant returns.
mcqs.explanation: The European Union published the list of seven safe countries on 16 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 17 April 2025, _____ Supreme Court removed the Taliban's designation as a 'terrorist organisation'.
mcqs.explanation: Russia's Supreme Court de-listed the Taliban as a terrorist group on 17 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 17 April 2025, _____ filed a challenge at the ICJ against Canada, Sweden, Ukraine and Britain.
mcqs.explanation: Iran filed an ICJ challenge against four states over the Flight PS752 shootdown.

mcqs.q: On 19 April 2025, Iran and the United States held their second round of negotiations in:
mcqs.explanation: The 2nd round of US-Iran nuclear talks was held in Rome on 19 April 2025.

mcqs.q: _____, the founder of the World Economic Forum, has resigned as Chair of its Board of Trustees.
mcqs.explanation: WEF founder Klaus Schwab resigned as Chair of the Board of Trustees in April 2025.

mcqs.q: The terror incident in Pahalgam (IIOJK) in which at least 26 people were killed took place on:
mcqs.explanation: The Pahalgam terror attack occurred on 22 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 23 April 2025, _____ outlawed the Muslim Brotherhood and confiscated its assets.
mcqs.explanation: Jordan outlawed the Muslim Brotherhood on 23 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 25 April 2025, _____ Foreign Minister raised his country's new flag at UN headquarters in New York.
mcqs.explanation: Syria's new government raised the new Syrian flag at the UN on 25 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 27 April 2025, Saudi Arabia and _____ announced settling Syria's $15 million World Bank debt.
mcqs.explanation: Saudi Arabia and Qatar jointly settled Syria's World Bank debt on 27 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On _____, India signed a contract to purchase 26 Rafale fighter jets from France.
mcqs.explanation: India signed the 26-Rafale Marine deal with France on 28 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 29 April 2025, the _____ won a new term in power after Canada's general elections.
mcqs.explanation: The Liberal Party (under Mark Carney) won Canada's April 2025 election.

mcqs.q: On 1 May 2025, President Trump replaced his National Security Advisor _____ following a chat-group leak.
mcqs.explanation: NSA Mike Waltz was replaced after the Signal chat leak.

mcqs.q: The United States and Ukraine signed a minerals deal on:
mcqs.explanation: The US-Ukraine critical minerals deal was signed on 30 April 2025.

mcqs.q: On 2 May 2025, an _____ boat-builder launched the world's largest electric-powered ship — Hull 096.
mcqs.explanation: Australian builder Incat launched Hull 096 on 2 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 3 May 2025, Salem bin Buraik was elected as the new Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: Salem Saleh bin Buraik became Yemen's Prime Minister on 3 May 2025.

mcqs.q: Lawrence Wong is the Prime Minister of:
mcqs.explanation: Lawrence Wong is the PM of Singapore.

mcqs.q: On 4 May 2025, Maldives President Mohamed Muizzu addressed a press conference for nearly 15 hours, breaking a previous record held by which country's president?
mcqs.explanation: Muizzu broke the previous record held by Ukraine's president (Zelensky).

mcqs.q: On 4 May 2025, _____ unveiled a new solid-fuel Ghassem Basir ballistic missile with a range of 1,200 km.
mcqs.explanation: Iran unveiled the Ghassem Basir missile on 4 May 2025.

mcqs.q: Ben Gurion Airport is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Ben Gurion Airport is located in Tel Aviv, Israel.

mcqs.q: The current US Secretary of Defense is:
mcqs.explanation: Pete Hegseth is the current US Defense Secretary.

mcqs.q: On 5 May 2025, Skype officially shut down, after nearly _____ years in operation.
mcqs.explanation: Skype shut down after roughly 22 years (founded 2003).

mcqs.q: On 5 May 2025, the ICJ threw out Sudan's case against the:
mcqs.explanation: The ICJ dismissed Sudan's genocide case against the UAE on 5 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 6 May 2025, _____ and India clinched a long-coveted free trade pact.
mcqs.explanation: Britain and India signed a free-trade agreement on 6 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 6 May 2025, Germany's conservative leader _____ won a nail-biter second vote in parliament to become Chancellor.
mcqs.explanation: Friedrich Merz became Chancellor of Germany on 6 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 8 May 2025, Cardinal Robert Prevost became the _____ Pope from the United States.
mcqs.explanation: Pope Leo XIV is the first US-born Pope in history.

mcqs.q: On 8 May 2025, President Trump announced a 'full and comprehensive' trade agreement with:
mcqs.explanation: Trump announced the US-UK trade deal on 8 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 9 May 2025, France and _____ signed a treaty committing both sides to mutual assistance in case of an attack.
mcqs.explanation: France and Poland signed a mutual-defence treaty on 9 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 12 May 2025, the United States and _____ agreed to temporarily slash reciprocal tariffs.
mcqs.explanation: The US-China temporary tariff slash deal was reached on 12 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 14 May 2025, the IMF reached an agreement with _____ to expand its multi-billion-dollar loan programme and authorise a $1.3 billion payout.
mcqs.explanation: The IMF agreement with Bangladesh — including the $1.3 billion payout — was reached on 14 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On _____, President Trump visited Qatar and the UAE.
mcqs.explanation: Trump's Qatar/UAE leg of his Gulf tour was on 15 May 2025.

mcqs.q: World Parkinson's Day is observed annually on:
mcqs.explanation: World Parkinson's Day is observed on 11 April.

mcqs.q: The Global Financial Stability Report is released by:
mcqs.explanation: The Global Financial Stability Report is published by the IMF.

mcqs.q: The first known world map is attributed to the _____ academic Anaximander.
mcqs.explanation: The Greek philosopher Anaximander is credited with the first known world map.

mcqs.q: Wadi Rum, sometimes referred to as the 'Valley of the Moon' or 'Valley of Mars', is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Wadi Rum is in southern Jordan.

mcqs.q: NASA collaborated with _____ to develop a standardised time system for the Moon.
mcqs.explanation: NASA partnered with the European Space Agency on the lunar time system.

mcqs.q: The Arctic region includes territories of _____ Arctic states.
mcqs.explanation: There are 8 Arctic states (members of the Arctic Council).

mcqs.q: On 16 May 2025, more than 40 leaders attended the European Political Community meeting in:
mcqs.explanation: The European Political Community summit was held in Tirana, Albania on 16 May 2025.

mcqs.q: Tirana is the capital of:
mcqs.explanation: Tirana is the capital of Albania.

mcqs.q: On 16 May 2025, the World Bank announced restart of its operations in _____, after a 14-year pause.
mcqs.explanation: The World Bank resumed operations in Syria on 16 May 2025.

mcqs.q: Per the Global Report on Food Crises, more than _____ million people faced acute hunger in 2024.
mcqs.explanation: The 2025 GRFC reported 295 million people in acute hunger in 2024.

mcqs.q: On _____, Pope Leo XIV held his inaugural mass in St Peter's Square.
mcqs.explanation: Pope Leo XIV's inaugural mass was on 18 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On _____, Britain and the EU reached a landmark deal aimed at forging closer ties on defence and trade.
mcqs.explanation: The UK-EU 'reset' deal was announced on 19 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 20 May 2025, surgeons in _____ successfully performed the world's first human bladder transplant.
mcqs.explanation: The world's first human bladder transplant was performed in Los Angeles on 20 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 20 May 2025, EU countries agreed to lift all remaining economic sanctions on:
mcqs.explanation: EU lifted all remaining economic sanctions on Syria on 20 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On _____, WHO member states adopted a landmark Pandemic Agreement on tackling future health crises.
mcqs.explanation: The WHO Pandemic Agreement was adopted on 20 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 21 May 2025, Iran's parliament approved the country's 20-year strategic partnership with:
mcqs.explanation: Iran-Russia 20-year strategic partnership ratified by Iran's parliament on 21 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 22 May 2025, the UK signed a contentious deal to return the remote Chagos Islands to:
mcqs.explanation: UK formally returned the Chagos Islands to Mauritius on 22 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 22 May 2025, the Trump administration revoked _____ right to enrol foreign students.
mcqs.explanation: The Trump administration revoked Harvard's authority to enrol foreign students on 22 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 24 May 2025, 'It Was Just an Accident' by _____ director Jafar Panahi won the Palme d'Or.
mcqs.explanation: Iranian director Jafar Panahi won the 2025 Palme d'Or.

mcqs.q: On 25 May 2025, _____ stripped more than 37,000 people, including at least 26,000 women, of citizenship.
mcqs.explanation: Kuwait revoked citizenship from over 37,000 people in May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 26 May 2025, the Palestinian delegation won the right to fly their flag at the:
mcqs.explanation: WHO authorised Palestinian flag flying on 26 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 27 May 2025, EU countries formally approved a new _____ billion euro loan programme to help rearm.
mcqs.explanation: The €150 billion SAFE rearmament loan programme was approved by EU countries on 27 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 29 May 2025, Witold Banka of _____ was re-elected as World Anti-Doping Agency President.
mcqs.explanation: Witold Banka of Poland was re-elected as WADA President.

mcqs.q: On 31 May 2025, _____ announced elevating its diplomatic post in Islamabad to full ambassadorial status.
mcqs.explanation: Afghanistan upgraded its Islamabad mission to full ambassadorial level on 31 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 31 May 2025, Opal Suchata Chuangsri of _____ was crowned Miss World 2025.
mcqs.explanation: Thailand's Opal Suchata Chuangsri was crowned Miss World 2025.

mcqs.q: On 1 June 2025, _____ officially accepted the Taliban's nomination of an ambassador to Moscow.
mcqs.explanation: Russia accepted the Taliban's nominee as ambassador to Moscow on 1 June 2025.

mcqs.q: On 1 June 2025, _____ began unprecedented elections allowing voters to choose their judges at all levels.
mcqs.explanation: Mexico began first-ever popular judicial elections on 1 June 2025.

mcqs.q: On 2 June 2025, Karol Nawrocki won the presidential election in:
mcqs.explanation: Karol Nawrocki won the 2025 Polish presidential election.

mcqs.q: On 3 June 2025, the UNGA elected five countries to serve as non-permanent UNSC members for 2026-2027 — Bahrain, Colombia, DR Congo, Latvia and:
mcqs.explanation: Japan was elected as a non-permanent UNSC member for 2026-2027.

mcqs.q: On 3 June 2025, Lee Jae-myung was declared the winner of a snap presidential election in:
mcqs.explanation: Lee Jae-myung won South Korea's snap presidential election on 3 June 2025.

mcqs.q: On _____, Sheikh Dr Saleh bin Abdullah bin Humaid delivered the Hajj 2025 sermon.
mcqs.explanation: The Hajj 2025 sermon was delivered on 5 June 2025.

mcqs.q: On 6 June 2025, Uzbekistan and _____ made history by qualifying for their first World Cup.
mcqs.explanation: Uzbekistan and Jordan both qualified for the FIFA World Cup for the first time.

mcqs.q: On 8 June 2025, _____ beat Spain on penalties to claim their second Nations League title.
mcqs.explanation: Portugal won the 2025 UEFA Nations League — their 2nd title.

mcqs.q: The UN Ocean Conference 2025 was held in _____, France.
mcqs.explanation: The UN Ocean Conference 2025 was held in Nice, France.

mcqs.q: Israel launched a strike against Iran on:
mcqs.explanation: Israel's Operation Rising Lion against Iran began on 13 June 2025.

mcqs.q: The Global Financial Stability Report is released by:
mcqs.explanation: The Global Financial Stability Report is an IMF publication.

mcqs.q: The first known world map is attributed to the _____ academic Anaximander.
mcqs.explanation: Anaximander was a Greek academic.

mcqs.q: The Intergovernmental Science-Policy Platform on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services (IPBES) was established in:
mcqs.explanation: IPBES was established in 2012.

mcqs.q: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) was established in:
mcqs.explanation: The IPCC was established in 1988 by UNEP and WMO.

mcqs.q: Emissions Gap Report, Adaptation Gap Report and Global Environment Outlook are published by:
mcqs.explanation: All three reports are published by UNEP.

mcqs.q: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) were adopted in:
mcqs.explanation: The 17 SDGs were adopted by the UN in September 2015.

mcqs.q: The Strait of Hormuz connects the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman and the _____ Sea.
mcqs.explanation: The Strait of Hormuz links the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea.

mcqs.q: On 14 June 2025, the two British personalities awarded knighthoods were:
mcqs.explanation: David Beckham and Gary Oldman were knighted in the King's 2025 Birthday Honours list.

mcqs.q: On 17 June 2025, MI6 appointed its first woman chief — the appointed leader was:
mcqs.explanation: Blaise Metreweli became the first woman Chief of MI6 (the 'C') on 17 June 2025.

mcqs.q: On 1 July 2025, the US Senate, led by Republicans, approved the domestic policy bill of:
mcqs.explanation: The US Senate passed Trump's 'Big Beautiful Bill' on 1 July 2025.

mcqs.q: Thailand's Constitutional Court suspended the Prime Minister from duty pending a dismissal case. The suspended leader was:
mcqs.explanation: PM Paetongtarn Shinawatra was suspended by Thailand's Constitutional Court.

mcqs.q: The first Indian actor to receive a Hollywood Walk of Fame star is:
mcqs.explanation: Deepika Padukone became the first Indian actor with a Hollywood Walk of Fame star.

mcqs.q: On 3 July 2025, the first country to formally recognise the Taliban government in Afghanistan was:
mcqs.explanation: Russia became the first country to formally recognise the Taliban government on 3 July 2025.
